title: The Capability Workbench
description: Double-Diamond kanban with five Claude action kinds per card. Personal + team-shared boards.
speakerNotes: Headline: this is where the user spends their time. The five lanes are the design-thinking pipeline. The five Claude actions per card are the ideation engine.

title: Proof: did the model see it coming?
description: Backtest harness aggregate accuracy + per-event breakdown across COVID, ChatGPT, SVB, tariffs.
speakerNotes: Note for the speaker: the harness measures model propagation under shock, not historical reconstruction — say this explicitly so the buyer doesn't misread the claim.

title: Marketplace + multi-tenant
description: Three seller tiers, four listing types, team-attributed purchases via Clerk org.
speakerNotes: CVI is the moat, marketplace is the revenue mechanism. Take rates: 20/15/12 across the three tiers.

title: The Wedge: Drop a doc, get a report
description: Drag-your-doc → capability extraction → streaming Vercel AI SDK report. The cold-prospect-to-diligence wedge.
speakerNotes: This is the conversion lever. The buyer never has to fill out a form to see value — they drop a doc and watch the report stream in.

title: VC/PE: Source, Comparables, Portfolio
description: Three surfaces that turn capability intelligence into the daily VC/PE workflow — sourcing, peer matching, and portfolio monitoring.
speakerNotes: Same capability graph powers all three. Source finds the target, Comparables vets it, Portfolio monitors it over time.

title: Network & Community
description: LinkedIn-style member graph layered on CVI — profiles, DMs, per-industry forums, three-column feed, hashtags + mentions.
speakerNotes: Capability intelligence is more useful with the people who own it. The network is the retention layer on top of the data moat.

title: Next Steps
description: Three engagement paths: Quick Assessment, Deep-Dive Workshop, Platform License
